gaps: The 'Modern discretionary-cert petitions by disposition' and 'by era' statpack sections that predict.md tells predictors to anchor on are absent from the committed metrics/statpack.md.
A query filter on docket-number range/prefix (paid vs IFP series, e.g. 92-5001+) would give the one cut that actually matters for sparse Term-numbered SCOTUS dockets.
Era-restricted retrieval is currently unusable: --era 1990s surfaces 19th-century material with misparsed modern-looking docket numbers.
helpful: statpack per-Term table — showed early-1990s resolved labels are too thin to lean on, which shaped calibration.
fedcourts paths — quick confirmation of resolved input/output locations.
Prior committed predictor runs under data/ as a formatting and calibration reference.
notes: Snapshot carried no caption, parties, or entries, so the prediction is a base-rate exercise; the docket-number convention (92-7301 = OT1992 IFP series) did all the case-specific work. Corpus query returned no usable priors for this era.
